title: User Profile Display, Edit Panel & Availability Calendar Integration

requirements.text: Extend the Motoko backend actor to store and manage full user profiles. The profile data model must include all fields collected across the existing registration forms: full name, email, phone/contact number, city, department, designation, years of experience, tribe/company name, role (Director or Production House), executive producers (for Production House), work reel URL, industry reference email, and availability dates. Expose CRUD endpoints: createOrUpdateProfile, getMyProfile, and updateAvailability. Profiles must be keyed by the caller's Internet Identity principal.

quotes: all information we have taken in the registration form from him
make it part of this feature

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores all registration form fields per principal
createOrUpdateProfile persists data and returns the saved profile
getMyProfile returns the authenticated caller's profile or a null/error if not registered
updateAvailability replaces the user's availability dates array
Data persists across canister upgrades via stable storage

requirements.text: After a user completes registration, display their profile avatar icon (initials-based circular avatar using their full name) and their full name in the AppLayout header, positioned immediately next to the Ad Tribe logo. This should only appear when the user is authenticated and has a saved profile.

quotes: Show an your profile icon with the users full name next to Ad tribe logo

acceptanceCriteria: Profile avatar and full name are visible in the header next to the Ad Tribe logo when user is registered
Avatar uses the user's initials rendered in a circular element with brand colors (saffron/forest)
Avatar and name are not shown for unauthenticated or unregistered users
Clicking the avatar or name opens the profile panel

requirements.text: Build a slide-over or modal profile panel that opens when the user clicks their avatar/name in the header. The panel must display all stored profile fields (full name, email, phone, city, department, designation, experience, tribe/company name, role, executive producers, work reel URL, industry reference email). Provide an Edit Profile mode that renders an editable form pre-populated with current values, with a Save button that calls the backend updateProfile endpoint and reflects the updated values on success.

quotes: on clicking the logo it should show all the registered details of the user with an option to edit his profile
all information we have taken in the registration form from him

acceptanceCriteria: Profile panel opens on avatar/name click and displays all registration fields in read-only view
An Edit button switches the panel to an editable form pre-filled with current values
Saving the edit form calls the backend and updates the displayed profile
Validation errors are shown inline if required fields are empty
Panel can be closed via a close/back button

requirements.text: Embed the existing AvailabilityCalendar component inside the user's profile panel as a dedicated 'My Availability' section. The calendar must load the user's currently saved availability dates from the backend and allow toggling dates to update them. Changes must be persisted to the backend via the updateAvailability endpoint when the user saves.
acceptanceCriteria: AvailabilityCalendar is rendered inside the profile panel below the profile fields
Calendar initialises with the user's saved availability dates fetched from the backend
User can toggle dates and save changes; updated dates persist to the backend
A Save Availability button (or auto-save) confirms the update and shows a success indicator
The calendar reflects the same data used by the Tribe Availability Enquiry feature

requirements.text: Wire the existing DirectorRegistrationForm and ProductionHouseRegistrationForm submission handlers to the new createOrUpdateProfile backend endpoint so that on successful form submission the user's profile is saved to the canister. After saving, navigate the user to the success page as currently implemented.

quotes: make it part of this feature

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the Director registration form saves all form data to the backend profile store
Submitting the Production House registration form saves all form data to the backend profile store
On successful save the user is navigated to the registration success page
After registration, the header immediately shows the user's avatar and full name

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Reuse the existing AvailabilityCalendar component; do not create a duplicate calendar
Profile data must be keyed by Internet Identity principal
nonGoals: Building a separate authentication system — Internet Identity remains the sole auth mechanism
Public profile pages visible to other users
Changing the existing Tribe Availability Enquiry form or its search logic
